channel.title: memos on daniel klotz |
channel.description: Recent content in memos on daniel klotz |

item.title: micro reviews
item.description: In the following you find some pretty random reviews of some pretty random things. Enjoy. Moving Pictures Fanatstic Mr. Fox. One of the best Wes Anderson Movies and in many ways the spiritual precessor of Isle of Dogs. Would recommend. The Grand Budapest Hotel. A gem. And probably, my favorite Wes Anderson movie. The whole movie is polished to the max. The story is touching, intense and comical. The colors are great.

item.title: model checking
item.description: Looking Inside The Blackbox. This is blog-post/tutorial about the usage of backprop do obtain some qualitative understanding of neural-networks. Not to brad, but I had a similar idea in the context of “data-assimilation for Deep Learning based rainfall-runoff modeling”. As in the blogpost, the idea was to backprop with regard to the inputs (instead of the parameters), but with the goal to get (a) a better forecast and (b) obtain a qualitative understand about what the networks thinks about the world.

item.title: goblin footnotes
item.description: Nasty patterns I found while dabbling in the research community. Experimental hollowness: When it becomes unclear if a benchmarking study, an empirical experiment or a simulation study contributes anything other than an improvement with regard to a under-specified metric (any perhaps even introduces with regard to some other relevant metrics). Empirical death end: When a theory does not admit any (more) experiments that can lead to its refutation or advancement.

item.title: regenerative tech
item.description: Classics Regenerative Design Environmental Engineering Environmental Design Ecological Engineering Howard T. Odum Human Ecology. Permaculture Ecosystem Services Degrowth Digital Permacomputing As far as I can tell permacomputing is so far mainly a nice essay that lays out a vision by the amazing viznut. Here is how it starts: Over the last few hundred years of human civilization, there has been a dramatic increase in the consumption of artificially produced energy.

item.title: analytic modeling
item.description: These notes are about using models to analyze things. Before getting lost in my notes and links I recommend starting here: First, read Bret Victor’s Open and Down the Ladder of Abstraction and Kill Math article. They set the tone for what these notes will explore. Second, check the follow-up notes by Michael Nielsen. I do not like them that much, but is still better than anything I could ever do.

item.title: multitudes
item.description: Actually model multitudes. Or manifolds? I am not sure. Right now the topic I have in mind seems to be so large for me to put down. Below you can find some sketches: Intuitions for higher dimensional thinking model weaving many models 2) Cheung et al. (2019): Superposition of many models into one. Mixing different model initialization to create a model that can adapt to different task. Below the first figure and the absract go get an idea.

item.title: paper extracts
item.description: A lot of smart things have been said. In the following is a small collection of quotes from scientific papers. Hence the title. I collect more condensed thoughts from the same domain at goblin foonotes. Sunset Salvo Some memorable quotes from John W. Tukey’s speech sunset salvo. My title is borrowed from Estil green who used it when he retired as Executive Vice-President at Bell Laboratories about two decades ago.

item.title: metrics 1
item.description: We only have sub-optimal metrics for reporting and evaluating model performance in rainfall-runoff modelling. This is not a controversial opinion (see for example Schaefi and Gupta, 2007 or Gupta and Kling, 2011). Many metrics implicitly assume some form of well behaved error distribution. In this series (?) I want to explore what happens if this is not the case by using simulation (using the NSE, the KGE and Pearson Correlation).

item.title: media diet
item.description: 30.Mai.2020 Very random one. Mostly some brain candy; some more, some less healthy. Dave Bull is a maker of japanes woodblock prints and intersting stories to tell. The muji fountain pen works great. I do not get how audible works. (comment: I do now & I dont like it. ) The Book Brood is a strange yet endearing youtube channel about … books and family life. The [green renaissance] makes captivating videos about individuals (e.

item.title: Rainy Meanderings
item.description: Here I will collect notes and references about the conceptualization in hydrology. Klemes (Conceptualization and scale in hydrology, 1986; https://doi.org/10.1016/0022-1694(83)90208-1) provides the basics. The abstract reads: It is argued that the levels of scale at which a meaningful conceptualization of physical processes is possible are not arbitrary and their range is not continuous. Formulations appropriate at a given level usually are not applicable at the immediately adjoining levels. This is seen as one of the important reasons for the slow progress of hydrological science on basin scale since it is dominated by different effects than those observed at the immediately lower level of channel reaches and hillslopes which represent the focus of current hydrological research.

item.title: Visualizing Uncertainty
item.description: I am not sure where one should start with this topic. Visualizing uncertainty is hard. in general, it is still an unsolved problem. Back in the days even the mighty Nathan Yau wrote: I still struggle with uncertainty and visualization. I haven’t seen many worthwhile solutions other than the old standbys, boxplots and histograms, which show distributions. But how many people understand spread, skew, etc? It’s a small proportion, which poses an interesting challenge.

item.title: Intro
item.description: Currently everything is very scrappy here and it will probably remain so for quite some time. I will try to put some pretty random notes on random things on here. The idea is that each “blogpost” will be expanded over time and is thus not finished. I may provide some kind of update news as the project goes along; that will however depend on how everything plays out. Some Definitions and Concepts scrap (/skɹæp/):
